docs.title: Policy Engine
docs.text: The policy engine is the core of PayGraph's spend governance. Every spend request — whether card or x402 — passes through the same PolicyEngine before reaching a gateway.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
request_spend() / request_x402()
        │
        ▼
   PolicyEngine.evaluate()
        │
   ┌────┴────┐
   │ Checks  │  amount_cap → vendor_allowlist → vendor_blocklist
   │ (ordered)│  → mcc_filter → daily_budget → justification
   └────┬────┘
        │
   ┌────┴────┐
   │approved │──▶ Gateway (card or x402)
   │denied   │──▶ PolicyViolationError
   └─────────┘

docs.title: SpendPolicy
docs.text:
SpendPolicy is a dataclass that holds all configuration:

Field Type Default Description max_transaction float 50.0 Maximum dollar amount per transaction daily_budget float 200.0 Maximum total spend per calendar day allowed_vendors list[str] \| None None If set, only these vendors are permitted blocked_vendors list[str] \| None None If set, these vendors are always blocked allowed_mccs list[int] \| None None Reserved for MCC filtering (future) require_justification bool True Whether a justification string is required

docs.title: Daily Budget Tracking
docs.text:
The PolicyEngine is stateful — it tracks cumulative daily spend in memory. The counter resets automatically at the start of each new calendar day.

from paygraph import AgentWallet, SpendPolicy

wallet = AgentWallet(policy=SpendPolicy(daily_budget=10.0))
wallet.request_spend(6.0, "Vendor A", "First purchase")   # OK ($6 used)
wallet.request_spend(3.0, "Vendor B", "Second purchase")   # OK ($9 used)
wallet.request_spend(2.0, "Vendor C", "Third purchase")    # DENIED (would exceed $10)

docs.title: Vendor Matching
docs.text:
Vendor matching is case-insensitive and uses substring matching:

  • Allowed vendor "AWS" matches "AWS Lambda", "aws s3", etc.
  • Blocked vendor "Casino" matches "Online Casino", "casino.com", etc.

docs.title: PolicyResult
docs.text:
Every evaluation returns a PolicyResult:

  • approved — Whether the request passed all checks
  • denial_reason — Human-readable reason if denied
  • checks_passed — List of check names that passed before denial (or all if approved)

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
• SpendPolicy
  • PolicyEngine

docs.title: Your First Policy
docs.text: Policies are the rules that govern what your agent can spend. Every spend request passes through the PolicyEngine before reaching a gateway.

docs.title: Default Policy
docs.text:
from paygraph import SpendPolicy

policy = SpendPolicy()
# max_transaction=50.0, daily_budget=200.0, require_justification=True

docs.title: Configuring Limits
docs.text:
policy = SpendPolicy(
    max_transaction=10.0,    # No single spend over $10
    daily_budget=50.0,       # Total daily spend capped at $50
)

docs.title: Allowlist — Only Permit Specific Vendors
docs.text:
policy = SpendPolicy(
    allowed_vendors=["Anthropic", "OpenAI", "AWS"],
)

When set, only vendors matching these names (case-insensitive substring match) are permitted.

docs.title: Blocklist — Block Specific Vendors
docs.text:
policy = SpendPolicy(
    blocked_vendors=["Casino", "Gambling"],
)

Blocks any vendor whose name contains a blocked term.

Note

You can use both allowed_vendors and blocked_vendors together. The allowlist is checked first.

docs.title: Justification Requirement
docs.text:
By default, agents must provide a justification for every spend. Disable it for automated pipelines:

policy = SpendPolicy(require_justification=False)

docs.title: Policy Check Order
docs.text:
The PolicyEngine evaluates checks in this order:

  1. Amount cap — Is the amount within max_transaction?
  2. Vendor allowlist — Is the vendor in the allowed list (if set)?
  3. Vendor blocklist — Is the vendor blocked?
  4. MCC filter — Merchant Category Code check (reserved for future use)
  5. Daily budget — Is there remaining budget for today?
  6. Justification — Was a justification provided (if required)?

If any check fails, the request is denied immediately and subsequent checks are skipped.

docs.title: Handling Denials
docs.text:
from paygraph import AgentWallet, SpendPolicy
from paygraph.exceptions import PolicyViolationError

wallet = AgentWallet(policy=SpendPolicy(max_transaction=5.0))

try:
    wallet.request_spend(10.0, "Vendor", "Too expensive")
except PolicyViolationError as e:
    print(f"Denied: {e}")
    # Denied: Amount $10.00 exceeds limit of $5.00

docs.title: Base Install
docs.text:
pip install paygraph

This gives you the core library with MockGateway for local development and testing.

docs.title: With LangGraph Integration
docs.text:
pip install "paygraph[langgraph]"

Adds langchain-core so you can use wallet.spend_tool and wallet.x402_tool in LangGraph agents.

docs.title: With Live Demo
docs.text:
pip install "paygraph[live]"

Includes LangGraph, LangChain Anthropic, and LangChain OpenAI for running the interactive demo with real LLMs.

docs.title: With x402 Support
docs.text:
pip install "paygraph[x402]"

Adds the Coinbase x402 SDK with EVM and Solana signers for on-chain USDC payments.

docs.title: For Development
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/paygraph-ai/paygraph.git
cd paygraph
pip install -e ".[langgraph]" pytest

docs.title: For Documentation
docs.text:
pip install -e ".[docs]"
mkdocs serve

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • For Stripe: a Stripe API key with Issuing enabled
  • For x402: an EVM or Solana private key with USDC balance

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Get from zero to a working spend-governed agent in 5 steps.

docs.title: 1. Install
docs.text: pip install paygraph

docs.title: 2. Create a Wallet
docs.text:
from paygraph import AgentWallet, SpendPolicy

wallet = AgentWallet(
    policy=SpendPolicy(
        max_transaction=25.0,   # Max $25 per transaction
        daily_budget=100.0,     # Max $100 per day
    ),
)

The default MockGateway prompts you for approval in the terminal — no API keys needed.

docs.title: 3. Make a Spend Request
docs.text:
result = wallet.request_spend(
    amount=4.20,
    vendor="Anthropic API",
    justification="Need Claude tokens for analysis",
)
print(result)
# Card approved. PAN: 4111111111111111, CVV: 123, Expiry: 12/28

docs.title: 4. Check the Audit Log
docs.text:
Every request is logged to paygraph_audit.jsonl:

{
  "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:30:00+00:00",
  "agent_id": "default",
  "amount": 4.2,
  "vendor": "Anthropic API",
  "justification": "Need Claude tokens for analysis",
  "policy_result": "approved",
  "checks_passed": ["amount_cap", "vendor_allowlist", "vendor_blocklist", "mcc_filter", "daily_budget", "justification"]
}

docs.title: 5. Try the CLI Demo
docs.text:
paygraph demo

This runs a fully interactive demo with a mock LLM and mock gateway. No API keys required.

For a live demo with a real LLM:

pip install "paygraph[live]"
paygraph demo --live --model anthropic
